This exporter sends OpenTelemetry metrics to [Google BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery)
using the [Storage Write API](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/write-api).

It creates one table per metric type (gauge, sum, histogram, exponential histogram, summary) and
is designed for analytics and dashboarding use cases (e.g., Looker, custom SQL).

## Configuration

```yaml
exporters:
  bigquerymetrics:
    project_id: "my-gcp-project"            # required
    dataset: "otel_metrics"                  # required
    location: "US"                           # optional, default: "US"
    create_schema: true                      # auto-create dataset/tables, default: true
    credentials_file: ""                     # optional, overrides ADC

    partition_granularity: "DAY"             # DAY | HOUR | MONTH, default: DAY
    partition_expiration_days: 0             # 0 = no expiration, default: 0

    metrics_table_name: "otel_metrics"       # base name, suffixes auto-appended

    timeout: 30s
    sending_queue:
      enabled: true
    retry_on_failure:
      enabled: true
```

### Required Fields

| Field        | Description                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------------|
| `project_id` | GCP project ID containing the dataset    |
| `dataset`    | BigQuery dataset name                    |

### Authentication

The exporter uses [Application Default Credentials (ADC)](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials)
by default. Set `credentials_file` to an absolute path to override with a service account key file.

**Minimum IAM roles required:**
- `roles/bigquery.dataEditor` — write data to tables
- `roles/bigquery.user` — run jobs

If `create_schema: true` (default), the service account also needs:
- `roles/bigquery.admin` — create datasets and tables

### Table Naming

Tables are named using the base name plus a metric-type suffix:

| Metric Type            | Table Name                              |
|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Gauge                  | `{metrics_table_name}_gauge`            |
| Sum                    | `{metrics_table_name}_sum`              |
| Summary                | `{metrics_table_name}_summary`          |
| Histogram              | `{metrics_table_name}_histogram`        |
| Exponential Histogram  | `{metrics_table_name}_exponential_histogram` |

### Partitioning

Tables are partitioned by `time_unix` using the configured granularity. Set
`partition_expiration_days` to automatically drop old partitions.

### Attribute Storage

Attributes (resource, scope, and data point) are stored as `JSON` columns. Use
`JSON_EXTRACT_SCALAR` or `JSON_VALUE` in BigQuery SQL to query attribute values.

> **Note on PII:** Attributes may contain sensitive data such as hostnames, IP addresses,
> or user identifiers. Use the OpenTelemetry Collector processor pipeline to redact
> sensitive attributes before they reach this exporter.
